channel.title: McKinsey Insights & Publications
channel.copyright: Copyright (c) McKinsey & Company. All rights reserved. Reprinted by permission.
channel.generator: Sitecore CMS: http://www.sitecore.net. Sitecore RSS module: McKinsey.RSS, Version=1.3.2.8186, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null

item.title: Global Economics Intelligence executive summary, July 2025
item.description: IMF revises global growth estimates upward but warns of ongoing trade tensions, while US and China record strong second-quarter growth; central banks in holding pattern on interest rates.

item.title: How CEOs are responding to geopolitical uncertainty
item.description: When the rules of global trade could change any day, how do you make decisions? McKinsey experts report from the business frontlines of geopolitical turmoil.

item.title: The rise of edge AI in automotive
item.description: AI is already talking to your car. Now automakers must decide how to harness its full potential.

item.title: McKinsey survey shows investors seek fundamentals and long-term vision
item.description: The latest investor survey underscores the importance of the right metrics and cohesive communications.

item.title: Rewiring aerospace and defense operations for speed and growth
item.description: To meet surging demand, manufacturers must urgently rethink their operations to unlock speed, focus, and frontline empowerment.

item.title: Europe’s next mobility leap may be driverless
item.description: Moia, a Volkswagen subsidiary, is preparing for an autonomous transition. CEO Sascha Meyer explains the real work: changing minds, not just tech.

item.title: The quantum revolution in pharma: Faster, smarter, and more precise
item.description: Quantum computing presents a multibillion-dollar opportunity to revolutionize drug discovery, development, and delivery by enabling accurate molecular simulations and optimizing complex processes.

item.title: Bias Busters: When the question—not the answer—is the mistake
item.description: Misframing a problem can lead to solving the wrong one.

item.title: General Motors’ Norm de Greve on aligning marketing across the C-suite
item.description: GM’s chief marketing officer shares how he builds partnerships across marketing and other executive functions to work toward a shared growth mindset.

item.title: Putting the pieces together: Unlocking success in modular construction
item.description: Developments in data, technology, and manufacturing are finally allowing modular to deliver on its promise. Insights from our database of more than 700 companies illustrate what success looks like.
item.category: Insights on Engineering, Construction & Building Materials

item.title: Wanted: A new economic playbook
item.description: Here is how India can reach consistent 8 to 9 percent real GDP growth required to become developed by 2047.

item.title: Clinical Trials Access Collaborative: Expanding clinical trial participation for all
item.description: With the support of McKinsey, the organization launched an expansion of clinical trial capacity at research sites across the United States, enabling more than 20 new trials and reaching over 4,600 participants to date.

item.title: Author Talks: How pop culture pitted millennial women against each other at work
item.description: Britney Spears. Bro films. The rise of reality TV. Aughties pop culture was tough on women—and continues to shape how millennials perceive ambition, workplace dynamics, and female leadership today.

item.title: Growing Australia’s productivity: Five big tests
item.description: McKinsey partner Ben Stretch and senior partner Chris Bradley discuss how Australia can kickstart its sluggish productivity to grow its economy.

item.title: Workday’s CEO on driving growth and transformation in the age of AI
item.description: Workday CEO Carl Eschenbach discusses agentic AI and building a durable growth company through a focus on service to others.

item.title: Tech faces a talent bottleneck: Here’s what to do about it
item.description: At first glance, the future of tech looks limitless: just think how fast artificial intelligence (AI), including generative and agentic AI, is advancing. Already, 80 percent of companies are using AI in at least one business function and more than 90 percent are planning to increase their AI investments, writes Kweilin Ellingrud in Forbes.

item.title: Closing the monetization gap in women’s sports: A $2.5 billion opportunity
item.description: US women’s sports have passed an inflection point and are poised for future growth, but stakeholders will need to overcome multiple challenges to deliver their full economic potential.

item.title: The paradoxes of Sweden’s success and struggles—and the path forward
item.description: Sweden can become a global leader but must resolve growth paradoxes through a bold, long-term agenda led by innovation, reform, and investment to accelerate economic growth.

item.title: Automotive product development: Accelerating to new horizons
item.description: New market entrants have cut automotive time to market in half. Established players need to speed up, or they risk being left behind.

item.title: The ‘octopus CIO’: Strategic technology leadership at Harvard
item.description: Harvard University CIO Klara Jelinkova describes the “soft power” needed to make sure that IT is delivering value.

item.title: Mind the gap: How operations leaders are pulling ahead using AI
item.description: A new study by MIT and McKinsey finds that AI investments in operations are paying back faster than ever, but leading organizations are pulling away from the rest.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Mickey Mouse Operation | No. 246
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Operations: The alpha factor in private equity carve-out deals
item.description: While negotiating carve-out deals, buyers and sellers should consider the operational risk—and price it in the valuation.

item.title: The case for a clear ROI framework for state transportation projects
item.description: US state departments of transportation are setting capital priorities against a backdrop where bankable plans—with the best returns on investment—are increasingly important for project funding.

item.title: Risk transfer: A growing strategic imperative for banks
item.description: Innovative risk transfer strategies can help banks optimize capital, unlock new revenue streams, and foster new partnerships with private investors. But the business also brings risks.

item.title: Doug Parker, former chairman and CEO of American Airlines, shares leadership lessons
item.description: The experienced airline CEO and board member reflects on leading through industry-defining disruption and the leadership lessons that endure.

item.title: The end of inertia: Agentic AI’s disruption of retail and SME banking
item.description: AI agents are poised to make sophisticated money decisions for customers, reshaping banking and affecting billions of dollars in revenue.

item.title: Propelling Indonesia’s productivity: Reaching high income by 2045
item.description: Indonesia has set an ambition to become a high-income economy by 2045. Is this possible? Two McKinsey experts discuss how it could be and what actions would need to be taken to achieve it.

item.title: Mobility and beyond: How autonomous technologies could transform lives
item.description: Tech investor Laurie Yoler shares her thoughts on the evolution of autonomous vehicles, changing mobility patterns, and the long reach of autonomous technologies.

item.title: Worst to first: What it takes to build or remake a world-class team
item.description: The winningest coaches, athletic directors, and administrators follow a few core principles to set uncompromising standards and build elite organizations. Here’s how C-suite leaders can do the same.

item.title: Reconfiguring work: Change management in the age of gen AI
item.description: Piloting gen AI is easy, but creating value is hard. That requires empowering employees to cocreate gen AI products against a North Star that redefines ways of working.

item.title: Mid-year update: Five surprises from China’s consumer market
item.description: Although macro indicators remain mixed and headwinds persist, several lesser-known developments are signaling deeper shifts in how China consumes, innovates, and competes both at home and on the global stage.

item.title: A bridge-building mentor, a Forbes ‘most-admired CEO,’ and a glass-ceiling-breaking economist
item.description: Our latest obituaries also include a chief automotive designer, a NASA engineer who saved lives, and an idealist “touched with fire.”

item.title: Closing the Black maternal-health gap: Healthier lives, stronger economies
item.description: Black American women face disproportionately high rates of maternal mortality and morbidity. Better research and care can transform health outcomes and boost the economy by $25 billion.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Human-Centric Leadership | No. 245
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: What’s trending in white goods? Durable, efficient, and affordable
item.description: How can OEMs navigate frugal consumers and shifting macroeconomics in the increasingly competitive white goods sector? We examine our latest global state of the industry customer survey to learn more.

item.title: How Uday Kotak uses dreams to manage risk and build value
item.description: Founder and director of Kotak Mahindra Bank, Uday Kotak, believes that common sense, an open mind, and strategic partnerships help build an institution that lasts beyond the individual.

item.title: Mapping the US affordable housing crisis and unlocking opportunities for economic mobility
item.description: Securing rising living standards for all Americans requires action on the affordable housing shortage.

item.title: Digital-led with a human touch: The next era in small-business banking
item.description: To fend off the rise of digital attackers, banks must serve MSMEs better with a model that effectively blends the best of digital and AI with the personal touch of an advisor.

item.title: What is RegTech?
item.description: RegTech, or “regulatory technology,” refers to technologies that improve processes at financial-services organizations, particularly those related to risk and compliance.

item.title: The data center balance: How US states can navigate the opportunities and challenges
item.description: State governments will need to balance a complex range of costs and a full view of the benefits as they weigh their strategies in data center development.

item.title: Rich in resilience: Dealmakers deliver strong first-half results in M&A
item.description: Nerves of steel or “same old, same old”? Either way, dealmakers stared down volatility, leading to higher global M&A activity in the first half of this year.

item.title: How to get your operating model transformation back on track
item.description: “All that work, and what did it get us?” Leaders feel frustrated when transformations fall short, but they can achieve their goals by avoiding six common pitfalls.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: How agentic AI can change the way banks fight financial crime
item.description: Financial institutions are allocating significant resources to fighting financial crime, but they are generally making little progress. AI-based solutions may be an accelerator.

item.title: Author Talks: The five ‘cross-pressures’ that define leadership
item.description: How can leaders learn from disruption and pursue purpose? Stanford lecturer in management Robert E. Siegel shares a systems leadership playbook and transforms classroom lessons into boardroom learnings.

item.title: Slowing data growth: A new telecommunications paradigm?
item.description: Annual mobile-data growth rates are expected to fall from more than 50 percent in 2019 to as low as 10 percent in 2029. Will this lead to unprecedented competition or a new profitable era for telcos?

item.title: Dr. Rupal Malani on the way forward for clinical-care organizations
item.description: McKinsey’s Dr. Rupal Malani discusses changing policies and workforce shortages and how leaders are using M&A and AI to improve margins, operations, and care delivery.

item.title: How Toll transformed procurement into an enterprise value driver
item.description: What does it take to lead a procurement transformation that delivers lasting impact in a world where the function is asked to do everything from managing volatility to generating sustainability and savings?

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Rather Iffy | No. 244
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Manufacturing in rural America: A plan for K–12–industry partnerships
item.description: A seismic threefold increase in advanced-manufacturing investment could transform rural America by driving economic growth, creating high-quality jobs, and revitalizing communities.
